mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as indicated by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflects a healthy economy with strong consumer spending potential, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is consistently positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative more than 55% of the time over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows a significant acceleration over a 3-month period, it signals increased pressure on the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL acceleration above 3.0% consistently coincides with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary conditions, which is highly favorable for S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If periods of strong retail sales and low federal funds rate consistently lead to negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, a significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a turning point in the labor market, which is a strong positive indicator for S&P 500 forward returns as economic recovery takes hold over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If a significant drop in UNRATE during high unemployment periods is followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing and overall economic output, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening construction activity and broader economic demand, often preceding a slowdown in economic growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls below 800 thousand units
failure_condition: If HOUST consistently falls below 800k units but S&P 500 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low, and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising, it indicates a late-cycle economy where the Fed is actively trying to cool an overheating economy, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If periods of low unemployment and rising FEDFUNDS consistently coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction (negative GDP growth), any positive growth in GDP (GDPC1) signals a potential bottoming or recovery, which is a strong positive catalyst for S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth turns positive after a negative quarter
failure_condition: If positive GDPC1 growth following a contraction is consistently met with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
